execution: compile offloaded via rch to remote worker; benchmarks RUN LOCALLY on this host for host-consistent timing
isolation: single-threaded library paths; shared host with peer agents (note: not isolated)
execution_host: thinkstation1 (LOCAL host == rch worker — same physical machine; rch 'offload' runs on the same box)

host_load_at_capture.note: 64 logical cores; ~45 load => heavily shared multi-agent box (~6000 procs). Single-thread benches get a core but suffer P-state jitter (powersave) + cache/sched interference.

measurement_validity: p50 (criterion median) is the PRIMARY trustworthy signal; p95/p99 are CONTENTION-INFLATED on this shared host and used only directionally. Ranking is by p50 ratio vs single-thread PyTorch + flamegraph attribution, NOT absolute p95.
governor_note: powersave + shared host — not tuned (skill rule: ASK before kernel/governor tuning). Documented, not corrected.
pinning: benchmarks run under taskset -c 62 to cut thread migration; other processes are NOT pinned away, so the core is still shared.
